# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses merge request title and description in build notifications and commit messages. This helps track which features are being deployed and generates automatic release notes. Include the MR title in deployment tags.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - setup
  - build
  - deploy
  - notify

variables:
  GIT_STRATEGY: fork
  GIT_BRANCH: $CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TARGET_BRANCH_NAME
  TAG: "$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE"

before_script:
  - echo "::set-env name=MR_TITLE::$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_TITLE"
  - echo "::set-env name=MR_DESCRIPTION::$CI_MERGE_REQUEST_DESCRIPTION"

setup:
  script:
    - apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl jq

build:
  image: your-image
  script:
    - build-your-project

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  when: on_success
  script:
    - tag_version () { echo "$TAG" > version.txt; }
    - curl -X POST --header "Content-Type: application/json" --data '{"tag": $(cat version.txt)}' https://api.gitlab.com/projects/${CI_PROJECT_ID}/tags
    - git config user.name "GitLab CI"
    - git config user.email "ci@yourdomain.com"
    - git tag $TAG
    - git push origin --tags
  only:
    - master

notify:
  stage: notify
  when: always
  script:
    - echo "Build completed for merge request ${MR_TITLE}" | gitlab-ci-lint comment
    - echo "Deployed to production: ${MR_TITLE}" | gitlab-ci-lint comment
